tokens: Let me get (my seatbelt) undone here.
Batteries are 90 (degrees) and 112, and the motors (are): forward left is off-scale low and right is 340.
Forward (means "left") rear is off-scale low, and right is 240.
I expect we've got a bad meter (on the forward right).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And you want to give me the bearing one more time there, Gene.
All I got was the distance (driven) at 9.1 and the range.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: When you're at the station, here's a couple of things...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...(charging ahead with rapid-fire instructions) we'd like for you guys to look at in the (housekeeping) overhead.
We'd like the TV lens to be dusted, in addition to the regular dusting.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) Can you try to tighten that (camera handle)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You might check the low-gain antenna elevation to make sure it's at 45 degrees.
We think you commented on that, and I think you're right now looking at tightening Jack's camera handle.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gene, you go ahead with the other...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, we are at 45 degrees (low-gain antenna elevation), Bob.
I'll lose the comm on you a second.
I've got to turn it towards me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, we'd like you to check the SEP for us.
I suspect we'll have to turn it off and open the mirrors and dust them.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Boy, when you get this (TV) picture...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...did not get a good bearing from you guys.
We might also check the LMP's camera.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll give it to you again.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; 071 is the bearing (to the SEP transmitter).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let's turn off the (SEP) power and the recorder, open the blankets, and dust it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Power's off; blankets are open; and, Gene, you'll have to dust it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got a lot of dusting to do here, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Looking at his checklist) Let's see what we've got to do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We've got a lot of housekeeping to do right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, I presume when I told you, you turned off the receiver, didn't you?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: My scoop just came off (the extension handle)!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; and Jack, and we'd like to get an EMU check on you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Jack, we'd like to go to India (Apollo magazine 138) on the magazine for you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: My goodness, we'll never get started.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, we are down in a dep(ression)...Look at where we came down, Jack.
And that was just one of the hills.
Got to go back up and then down some.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) Hey, thank you for that fix on the fender, by the way, because I'd hate to see what it would look like without it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And John (Young) suggests that we might just check it momentarily while you're here to make sure it's still holding on good and tight.
Both the clamps and the tape.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) If it stayed on through that ride it may never come off!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got to get this SEP.
Do you want me to dust the SEP, is that what you said?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Do you want the covers open?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: They should be open and dusted.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: My camera look all right to you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get yours; lean over here, and I'll get yours.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, we're suggesting that you're getting a little warm.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I feel the same way; but I want to get this camera fixed.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Joking with Houston) Can I change your oil?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about any other service I can be?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Talking louder to Bob) Okay, Houston, the number of blocks plotted on the map are not nearly enough.
In the greater than 1-meter range, there are many hundred blocks on the massif flank of Nansen (that is, on the south side of Nansen) and up around Station 2, where we are.
There are only one or two blocks on the light mantle side of Nansen.
It looks as if the material in the bottom of Nansen is overriding the light mantle materials of the north wall (of Nansen).
They (the South Massif talus)'re slightly lighter albedo than the north wall of Nansen.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I suggest that we do our raking...(Responding to Bob) That's right; I just told you everything you can see...(I suggest that we do our raking) fairly close to the Rover to get sort of the general population of talus material coming off the massif.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, on my mark...I've got everything: hammer, gnomon, film.
Mark it; you have a gravimeter measurement going.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: They're multilithic, gray-matrix (pause), matrix (dominated) breccias, I guess.
There are fragments in them, but it doesn't look like more than about 10 or 15 percent fragments.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Some of the light-colored fragments seem to have very fine-grained dark halos around them.
The zap pits (in the dark matrix) do not have white halos, so I suspect they are not crystalline (rocks).
They might be the vitric or glassy breccias.
At least, the one big rock we have here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a very rough foliation in them (the blue-gray breccias) - and I'm not sure - it's shown by the elongate knobs on the surface.
It looks like a fracture foliation of some kind.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, that rock has almost got to have come down (from the outcrops high on the mountain), don't you think?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll bet you it's the same as the blue-gray rocks we see up higher.
Here's some more blue-gray ones over here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Look at the size of some of these light fragments in here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, but it still...It looks like they're dominantly matrix breccias.
There are light-colored fragments, and they (the fragments) may be crystalline.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They're very light colored; they look like the shattered anorthosites.
They have white halos (around zap pits)...I think that's what those fragments are.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, let's get a piece of this one right here (Boulder 1).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This is the blue-gray (breccia) variety, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to take that little knob off up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; well, you can work that block over...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We ought to sample across that layering...that foliation.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: When you look down into the bottom of Nansen, it looks - like, I guess, would sound obvious - that some of the debris that has rolled off of the South Massif covers up the original material there that covers the north wall of Nansen.
You've got that very wrinkled texture in the north slopes of Nansen, and you've got the South Massif debris in the south slopes of Nansen.
And the debris, of course, overlays the north slope.
And all the rock fragments, all the boulders that have come down, are all on the south side of the slope of Nansen.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I take back what I said about no (zap pit) halos.
There are light - not very sharply light - but light halos around zap pits in the matrix.
The matrix glass is dark, and it seems to have a greenish cast; but it's very dark.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Look at the white fragments in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me come and help you there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, there's some boulder rolling rocks here, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's an overhang we've got to get into.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; (sample number) 514 is the...Okay, I'll take it back.
On the fresh surface, these look like fragment (dominated) breccias although the fragment size is fairly small.
There are dark gray fragments and the light fragments we talked about.
The gray ones are very fine grained and dense, although I see flashes that indicate they may be crystalline.
The light-colored fragments are as I described earlier, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...if you could tear yourself away in the middle of that sometime to give us an EMU readout, we'd appreciate it.
We haven't gotten that from you yet on the EVA.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gene's got a rock to go (in a sample bag).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The first rock was from about...(Sample number) 514 was from a meter above the base of the rock; 515 is from about a meter and a half.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Here, can I get this in your (SCB)...(Pause) Can you get some on either side of those two now?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll leave you open for a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just so they don't fall out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get this other one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me try from back here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Of course, that's a north/south overhang.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, you're facing right into the east.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know if I can get a piece back here or not.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Let me set my working tool (the hammer) down here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Once you get it on there, you might as well forget it.
494 is from a half a meter above the base of the rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And these are samples from across the layering...
(Stops to listen) These are samples from across the foliation.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Can you get that one up there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I might either get that or this other piece up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't reach it without my camera hitting (the boulder).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) Okay, this next sample...(To Gene) Can you get a bag out, and we'll try to put it around it.
This is a light-matrix breccia; whereas the other three fragments were dark-matrix or dark-fragment breccias.
The big rock (fragment that Jack is discussing now) is a light-matrix breccia with dark fragments, and it's the one that has the (dark) halos around the light fragments.
It's just...495 is wrapped around it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's not going to stay, is it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why don't you see if you can stuff it in there...with the bag down.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, we'll be able to identify 495 when we get back.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Is the bag on it now?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, yeah,...It's on it, facing down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...do you guys see any tracks coming down to these boulders?
Do have any feeling that you can place (the source of) these that way?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The main tracks are out into Nansen, and I don't think we can get over there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; that's those biggies that we see on the maps, huh?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Coming up, I was looking; and there are no obvious tracks coming down here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Wait a minute; that gnomon is probably not...Well, that's right; you got stereo earlier.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The gnomon was moved a little between the samples.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do you need to take a vertical pan?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get a flight line this way.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you get a locator from here, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got flight line on the north/south trend; Gene got east/west.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You going to get that sample under there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, we got to get the soil.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And look at that, that rock is fragmented; let's see, it's southeast/northwest.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that one right over there is okay.
Hey, did you want to get this?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Houston) This is a fillet from underneath the rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And an update on the rake samples when you get around to it.
We'd like to get one up on the massif slope as much as you can, if you can get over to it.
And then the second one down near the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: This fillet is (from) up underneath an overhang.
I got it from about...(To Gene) I got to get uphill from you a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) It's about...(To Gene) That's good.
(To Bob, finishing the thought)...oh, a third of a meter under an overhang.
And it's the upper 3 centimeters of soil.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now let me get one out away from the overhang a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And a sample down to a depth of about 5 centimeters, about two-thirds of a meter from the boulder, on the south side, is in 497.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, let me get a skim sample, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got to take a set of pictures after that, by the way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can piece them into my flight line stereo.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They were in both of the "before" pictures on those rocks.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Boy, that's hard on the hand (manipulating the scoop) even in one-sixth g.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I didn't park that Rover in a very good spot for them to watch what's going on, I guess; but that was the heading.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We didn't work in the right spot; that's all.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Every now and then we get a peek at you guys.
But only every now and then.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You know, that's the way it happens.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I didn't give it to you; but I think...Well the next bag I take out, you can check the num(ber)...Well, wait a minute, I'll do it for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Not having heard Bob yet) I'm almost positive it was 498.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, looking at the blocks directly down-Sun, the gray-matrix breccias seem to be fragments, or schlerin anyway (elongated fragments with vague boundaries, usually elongated by either compression or shearing), within the white-matrix breccias.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I got a couple pictures down-Sun to show that texture.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And one thing we'd like to do would be to sample a variety of blocks, in terms of looking at differences in the blocks...from block to block.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're going after a lighter-colored block, now.
(To Gene) Are you going up there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; and if you're going up the Massif, why don't we try and get the rake sample up there now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, Jack...Jack, don't come up here unless you bring the rake.
No sense coming up here twice.
I can go get this sample.
I'd get the rake, if I were you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I don't...(Contemplative mumble) I'm not sure they're going to gain anything by coming up to the top (higher than the Rover).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You're not going to gain a thing, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (He decides not to waste time arguing) The rims of the small craters in the talus are softer than the normal terrain.
My foot goes in maybe 10 centimeters where normally it only goes in a centimeter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: As long as it's above the break of the slope, Jack, we don't have to get very far up the slope.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Jack, since you're back at the Rover, how about giving us a Grav reading before you leave.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Because I'm late sampling, that's why.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'm at another boulder up the slope here.
It looks quite similar to the one we just sampled, except there is a lot of flake fractures on it.
Non-uniform, non-directional, but quite different, at least from that other rock, in terms of its fracture pattern.
The texture looks to be quite similar.
Boy, I'm glad I don't have to walk to the top of this thing (the South Massif).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, look, Gene, on these rake samples, there is just no point in carrying a rake all the way up here...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Negative, Jack, as long as you're above the break...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...because all we needed was a break in the slope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: As long as you're above the break in the slope; that's right.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's being done; but let's watch those kind of calls please.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Acting the peacemaker, smoothing ruffled feathers) They can't appreciate the toughness of going up this slope, though.
We can; we've got to tell them that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that's what we were saying.
Don't go above just at the base of the break in the slope, Jack.
Don't climb all the way up there with it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We want to get away from that big rock (to get the rake sample) because it's probably shedding.
Hey, that's a different rock, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, it looks like the same texture, but it's got that flaky fracture pattern all over it.
I'm going to get a (flightline) stereo while I'm at it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This ought to cover any samples I take off of that thing.
(Pause) I'm going to get myself a zap of cold water.
(Pause) Man, we've got to be a million miles away from the LM.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, this is a crystalline rock, Houston.
It's got nice white halos around the zap pits.
The (centers of the) zaps are not dense black glass, but a dark greenish...a very dark greenish-gray.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, there are fragments, I think, also.
But, right now, (I'd say) it's fairly crystalline, but it is heterogeneous.
Matter of fact (laughs) there's a big fragment of a porphyry caught up in this thing, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you get a locator, by any chance?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I want to start taking some (samples).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gene, we got to get some of that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's where I'm going right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And there's a chunk there we can get.
That's a big fragment within this crystalline rock,...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Take a picture of that and then your locator, I'll get it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got it (that is, the locator).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, it does look like a crystalline rock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like an andesite porphyry is what it looks like.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Garbled) got some very large crystals in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Houston) It's a relatively angular inclusion.
It's about a half a meter in size, and it's a square cross section.
Well, it's irregular; but generally square cross section.
It's in bag 516, and it looks like it's a high feldspar rock.
It may be an anorthositic gabbro, but it does look like a porphyry.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a big chunk where I've got...I can't get it out, though; it's buried in the rock...Oh, a half-an-inch, elongated...I can't see whether they are colorless or not, but they are certainly reflective crystals.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And then in the big rock, you've got massive things - like this big fragment here - that's 5 inches across.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, that may be a spall point, Gene; that's a lighter color, in general, because of a zap or something.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get some more samples off it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, we need to get some of the host rock here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay now, you're still sampling the one we just got.
So we'll get another one (of the same inclusion).
The same kind...The contact of that (inclusion with the host) rock looks like it might be finer grained, but it's about the same...in 517.
That's the inclusion side of the contact.
(Pause) Keep going after the other one, Gene, I'll get this in your bag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, you could probably see this rock if you look over this way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Quite a sight; quite a goodie.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me see if I can't get this one here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The host rock for the inclusion - which appears to be also crystalline but may be a re-crystallized rock of some kind...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (of) metamorphic...(It) also looks like it's (got a) high plagioclase (content)...high feldspar, anyway.
And that was a fairly loose but in-place fragment along a fracture zone.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Will you hold this a minute?
I'm going to try to get the rest of it up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And for your thinking in the next few minutes, you might also factor in the question the Backroom raises about taking 10 minutes out Station 4 and adding it into this station, given the wealth of interest that seems to be occurring here.
You haven't been to Station 4, so it's a little hard to judge.
But if you think 10 minutes can be very profitably spent, you might as well do that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, we'll think about it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't get any more of that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...anorthositic gabbro, and it looks like it has some pastel-green olivine crystals in it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't get any more of it, Jack, up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and that small chip of that is in 519.
It's the same host rock, much like the previous sample.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a good sample for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) And another chunk of the host...Oops, be careful.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I need to get rid of this (sample)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I haven't closed your bag (Gene's SCB) yet.
(Pause) And we've got to get one soil sample up the hill here.
Oh, we're going to get the rake...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We ought to get a soil sample, though, up here, so...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll get the rake sample right over here on this slope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Having just chipped off a rock fragment) Where did that thing go, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Was that last sample in 518, as well?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We haven't put it in yet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Houston) Bob, this is a fairly uniform-looking rock.
It does have some widely-spaced fractures across it.
It's clearly crystalline and has crystalline inclusions in it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Might get the soil from around that thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, both rocks look like they might be in the anorthositic class...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Your bag is still open part way, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's just that one has the appearance of being a finer grain matrix.
Looks like a porphyry in the boulder.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And a reminder, as you photograph it, to remember that the photograph in the southwest quadrant there will be the best ones.
Around the corner on two sides there will be the best ones to show the structure through the whole rock.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Thinking about what Bob has just said) Oh, the southwest?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The southwest face...Or it faces not quite south.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll just continue my stereo around here.
Hey, Jack, you can get way under there, and I know you could get soil.
I don't know how long it's been shadowed, but it's been shadowed as long as this rock's been here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got to stereo this one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'm getting it from this way, and they like that.
Did we kick any dirt in under there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't...No, I don't think so.
Let me get a couple of "after" pictures.
Yeah, we want to get two sides of these rocks, and you can see their structure.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and if I could remind you guys to get a pan...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...from up there before you leave the high uphill area there.
There's no point in climbing up there twice.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: How much time we got here now?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We got 12 or 13 minutes left at this station; unless you take that extra 10 minutes that we were offering you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We got to get the rake.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's take it; we'll need it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me try again (to get the overhang sample).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know whether I can or not.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do you know how far under you're getting, by any chance?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Houston) I got under an east-west overhang about 20 centimeters...Way back.
Quite a ways back; it goes even farther, but that's about as far as I can reach back and sample
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Isn't that pretty over...Can you see the Massif, too?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now we're coming down to look at the Massif.
Had a beautiful picture of the Pacific there?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Probably having stowed the sample in Gene's SCB) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Bob, I took an "after" picture (AS17-137-
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Are you through with the gnomon?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll set it up for the rake.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll go up there and get a pan, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I didn't get that soil bag number, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're on a pretty good slope, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Gene) Hey...Bob, how long have we been at this station?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) You've been here about forty minutes right now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And we're going to give you that extra 10 minutes out of Station 4.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I can't believe we've been here...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That leaves you about 20 minutes; then you'll have to be moving.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Boy, this pan may be looking right smack in the sides of the Massifs.
Only way you can get it is to lean back, and I can't lean downhill.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Warning Jack and making a mis-identification) Hey.
Watch out for that crater behind you there, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm standing in the crater so I can get level.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I have some good pictures of Nansen, anyway, and...(Long Pause) You know, I look out there, I'm not sure I really believe it all.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, my down-Sun pictures on the rake were taken at f/8.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This isn't an easy (task)...Okay, I got to get out of my shadow or I can't see what I'm doing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll be right down there to bag that rake (sample) for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got to get it first.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Laughing and joining Jack) Man, I tell you; can you come downhill in a hurry.
Going uphill is a nice job.
Bob, I'd say we can meet our walkback constraints, if anyone's interested.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I expect it's all downhill from here.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Can you guys see the LM, or are you too far down to see the LM?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...that's why I said we could meet our walkback constraints.
The LM is over about three rises in the Scarp before we can even see it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I thought that might have happened.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm not even at a level of the last hill we came over.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know if you've looked up that way (with the TV).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We had a feeling for that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We can meet them (the walkback constraints), but I wouldn't stretch them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Not many small, walnut-sized fragments in here, Bob.
Gotten about seven or eight (in the rake sample).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, there aren't a lot; but that'll fill up a bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll have to look (at the checklist); I think so.
I think they all are, aren't they?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And this is one that we would like to get the kilogram of soil from, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll use my scoop for that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, what do we have left here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Consulting his checklist) We want to get a...I got the high pan.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know how we used up all the time, but we did.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, (on) my pan, by the way, I got extensive vertical coverage down into Nansen, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, the soil's getting into my (scoop hinge)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know where the hour went that it took to drive here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know whether that makes us older or not.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And that's a sample down to about 5...about 4 centimeters.
Don't get (the sample bag) too close to your camera.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, that's a big bag full.
Want to put it in mine?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't feel it (that is, the weight of the SCB).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And guys, do you see any more different blocks up there that are worth sampling before you go on down on to the flats (down near the Rover, below the break in slope) and sample the light mantle?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We haven't had a chance to look around any more than you've heard.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You want a rake in the light mantle here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We want a rake in the light mantle.
You might as well get that down by the Rover later on.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) Get an "after" (photo)...Get an after, Gene.
(Calling after him) Gene, get an after.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Got it, got it, got it, got it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...for a couple of documented samples there, up on the slope of the Massif, before you move down the flatter, light mantle areas by the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack got the "befores" on the rake and I got the "after".
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, here are two rocks side by side, a meter or two in diameter.
And one is the anorthositic gabbro, if I can use the term; and the other is that two-cycle breccia.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, that's the way to come downhill.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Cernan : Yeah, that's the way to come downhill.
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: Let's get that...Let's get that big clast.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a fracture right in there I want to get the...Oh, the clast.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Very pleased by Jack's discovery) Yes, sir.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Big white clast in the two-cy(cle)...(correcting his terminology) in the gray-matrix breccia.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get this over here so I can (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think you can even get it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I want to get this big...Yeah, I think I can get that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't believe the trouble I have with f-stops.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, I want to try to take this piece off first.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's the same clast over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you use your blade end?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, let me get that little piece, anyway, to start with.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Grabbing the fragment with the scoop) Got it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Before we cover them up, let's get them.
I got to get a sample of that mother rock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, Let me see if I can't get a sample...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Want to try to hit that one more time.
I think we've got another one coming there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That looks almost like a rhyolite from here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, that's not going to come off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, this is a fine-grained, but crystalline, white clast in the (blue-)gray breccia; and it's mixed with soil.
We had to pick up a little soil.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I guess they're all there, aren't they?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There are three clasts, anyway...(Correcting himself) three fragments that we got off.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get a piece of the rock it's in.
And I'm going to take a close-up stereo of that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I tried to catch it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Look at the size of the piece that came off there, though, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got another piece of it up here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, the host rock for that inclusion of white material will be in bag...What is it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just make sure they're closed so they don't...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I wore my hand out holding that camera together coming out here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're getting some samples this time.
I want to get an "after", and I want to get a close-up stereo of that.
And I'm going to get some pictures around this block, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's an "after" and now I'm going to get sort of a close-up stereo around it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Bob, while he's doing that, there's a real good example of a pit-bottom crater up here even on this talus slope.
I'll try to take a stereo of it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I guess there's always a problem of getting the in-place glass, if you think that's appropriate at this point.
Word along those lines, though, is we'd like to have you in the Rover moving in 11 minutes; so it's probably not appropriate at this time on that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, there isn't any glass in this crater.
(Gesturing) You can see it with your TV.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's just bigger than the average crater.
And it still has that pit, the pit being about a third of the inner diameter of the crater...make it a fourth of the rim diameter, that's easier.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Can I look at that closely?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We got to be moving in how many minutes, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'd like to have you moving in one-zero (10) minutes, which means: allow the usual 3 or 4 or 5 minutes for close-out before that time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, that white-colored inclusion we sampled looks like a strange...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How about getting a soil sample under there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Get that sample under there, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The soil from right underneath the rock - down to about 4 centimeters (depth) - (is going) in 505.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I'll try to skim it here a little, too.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, this big white clast, I'm not sure there aren't some smaller ones in some of those other big boulders.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But we never saw any as obviously big and gross as this one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Fact is, this particular boulder I photographed, I had three of them other than the one we sampled.
And that's 505 and 506, in that order.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And by now, probably the best thing for you guys...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...to do is to go back to the Rover and pick up the rake sample.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That...(Turning uphill to get the rake) (To Gene) I'll get it (the rake).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) That white clast...I looked at it, and it has light, pastel-green, fairly-rounded crystals in a fine-grained white to light pinkish-tan matrix.
And you can figure that one out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It might be a...(Responding to Bob) No, the colors aren't that distinct, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Bob, have you panned down into Nansen and seen this rock that's, oh, 30 or 40 meters from us?
To give you an idea of the kind of upslope filleting you have on some of those boulders.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll send Ed (Fendell) over there to look at it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I'll help him (by manually pointing the TV).
I don't think you got enough time.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we'd like you guys to get going on the rake sample.
We'd like light mantle on the rake there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Talking to Gene under Bob) Where's your gnomon?
We've got to get a rake sample.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Headed north from the Rover) I'm going to have to move out here a ways, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Right there is what I'm looking at.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're going to check it out; thank you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Getting the gnomon out again) And there's no sense trying to get 500's up...Well, we'll see what happens.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Also, there's no time to get 500's either, unfortunately.
We're planning on Station 4, which will be a better perspective distance anyway.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I was going to say there's no sense in trying to get them up the Massif; I don't think you'll see anything up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Where do you want it (the gnomon for the rake sample)?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, right over there where there's some fragments.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and then I'll get the "down-(Sun)".
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me tell you, you just got to think an order of magnitude bigger than what you're normally are accustomed to thinking.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let's get the rake sample so we can move on.
(Pause) Bob, I couldn't get those 500's anyway.
It would require me to pitch up (lean back) too far, and there's no way I could do it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, we're definitely not in favor of that, Gene, at this area.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm just mulling it over, but there really isn't any way I could get them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me rake that a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, it's all right; there just aren't any rocks.
Should have brought the scoop and used the old shovel trick.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, do you have any feeling (that) you have that hard layer underneath there like you did yesterday, when you raked at Station 1, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: There's one under the gnomon you can get.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Several I thought were rocks turned out to be clods.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, that's what most of them are is clods.
How do you get clods if it's never been wet?
You've had three in there ever since the last four scoops.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, you got about four rocks about 2 inches and smaller.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let's just get the soil and...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And we want to get the soil.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'd like to move in 3 minutes, 3 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, you got it (the down-Sun picture)?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me put this in your bag (SCB) and...Forget the soil.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: He wants us moving in 3 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Don't forget the soil; get the soil.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Hearing Bob) I'm sorry, I thought you said to skip it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You'll have to start putting some of these samples in my bag.
You're getting a full bag for Christmas here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Is it so full we ought to change it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's do that after we get to the next station, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We ought to start moving out of here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get one "after" of the area.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Beautiful station, guys; just simply beautiful.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I couldn't help that, Bob; it's just too beautiful.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Jack, will you look where we kicked up this stuff.
There's some light...Well, I can't see it now, I'm looking...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's a light-colored fragment (means "layer") I think we break into.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And when I was walking uphill, I really wasn't sinking in - probably - more than an inch or two.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want to take this bag off of me?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Because we want to get rolling.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, 17, there's a couple of things here, while your getting undone there.
There's our housekeeping to close out.
We'd also like to get the SEP turned on, and you might read us the temperature when you turn it on.
And other than that, stowing the TV and low-gain (meaning to say "high-gain") antenna and you're on your way.
We've taken care of the gravimeter already.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Did our (gravimeter) reading change much, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Gene) Make sure that's locked on there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, it is locked; make sure the cap's locked.
(To Bob) Okay, bag 8 is on the gate, and Jack's getting bag 4.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, I know my camera's going to be...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You copy on the SEP receiver turn on and temperature?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Bob) We got that, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 17, take all that back, we've just had a change of heart back here.
And we're not going to turn the SEP on, just cover it up.
And you might give us a temperature reading as you go by; that'll help us think what to do with it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Copy 98; then leave them both off.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: John (Young) and Charlie (Duke) are kind of advising you to put that full SCB(-8) underneath the seat to make sure the top doesn't bounce open and lose some of those rocks.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, you can't take better advice than from those who have been here!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Their advice has been pretty good, so far.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I won't pass that on to them.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: These locks (on the tool gate) are clamming up (with dust), Jack.
I can't unlock that one now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That one just didn't want to work any more.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me try once more, if I have to...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, those are really getting dusty.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Charge that time up to John and Charlie!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got to get the (TV) camera.
Okay, Bob, I'm taking your camera.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, looks like it's in the right place, you won't have to turn it around.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Long Pause) And give me a call when you guys get rolling.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And we'd like frames when it's convenient on you guys.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And CDR is at - if I stop long enough - 113.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
